review_new_balance.approach: Near-mastery frontier softening: count topics with 1+ consecutive correct in Review state as mastered for prerequisite checks
review_new_balance.result: Barely moved metric (0.870→0.864), caused interleaving PASS→WARN regression (0.096→0.107)

review_new_balance.approach: Aggressive review cap (70%→60%) + new topic cap (3/session) + review proportion (60%→50%)
review_new_balance.result: Interleaving regressed PASS→FAIL (0.096→0.212), review ratio barely improved (0.870→0.851)

fire_compression.approach: Fixed inverted discounting + increased cap to 150% + full weight (no retrievability discount)
fire_compression.result: Metric still -5.8% (negative compression). FIRe due-date extensions without updating FSRS state cause FSRS to interpret longer gaps as memory decay, increasing review frequency.

fire_compression.approach: Disabled child removal from compressReviews remaining set
fire_compression.result: Worsened to -10.5%. More children appearing as explicit reviews while also getting extensions.
Still -10.5%. Fundamental issue: extending due dates confuses FSRS state model. Needs architectural redesign (virtual FSRS reviews).

epoch_snapshots.fix_description: Lowered mastery criterion: Path A from 3 consecutive correct + stability>=7 to 2+stability>=4, Path B from 5 to 3 consecutive correct. Accelerates frontier unlocking by mastering topics faster.
Two approaches tried, both caused regressions. Frontier exhaustion (~27 topics over 30 sessions) makes 0.50-0.70 target mathematically difficult without more content. Skipping for now.

Three approaches tried, all failed. FIRe due-date extension model fundamentally conflicts with FSRS state tracking — extending due dates without virtual FSRS reviews causes FSRS to interpret gaps as memory decay, increasing review frequency. Needs architectural redesign.
